text.arabic:
ذَهَـبَ الظَّمَـأُ،
وَابْتَلَّـتِ العُـروق،
وَثَبَـتَ الأجْـرُ إِنْ شـاءَ الله
text.transliteration:
Dhahabaẓ-ẓama',
wabtallati ‘l-`urūq,
wa thabata ‘l-'ajru in shā Allāh.

translations.en:
The thirst is gone,
the veins are moistened,
and the reward is confirmed, if Allah wills.

items.reference:
Abu Dawud 2/306 and others. See also Al- Albani, Sahihul-Jami' As-Saghir
4/209.

text.arabic:
اللّهُـمَّ إِنَّـي أَسْـأَلُـكَ بِرَحْمَـتِكَ الّتي وَسِـعَت كُلَّ شيء،
أَنْ تَغْـفِرَ لي
text.transliteration:
Allāhumma innī as'aluka
bi raḥmatika ‘l-latī wasi`at kulla shay'
an taghfira lī.

translations.en: O Allah, I ask You by Your mercy, which encompasses all things, that You forgive me.

items.reference:
Ibn Majah 1/557 from a supplication of Abdullah bin 'Amr. Al-Hafidh graded
it as good in his checking of An-Nawawi's Kitabul-'Athkdr. See Sharhul-
Athkar 4/342.
